skill.name: global-commenting
skill.description: Write minimal, evergreen code comments that explain complex logic without documenting obvious behavior or temporary changes. Use this skill when adding comments to PHP files, TypeScript/JavaScript files, or any code files, when documenting complex algorithms or business logic, when adding PHPDoc blocks or JSDoc comments, when writing self-documenting code with clear naming, or when reviewing existing comments for relevance and necessity. Focus on keeping code self-explanatory through clear structure and naming rather than relying heavily on comments.
skill.summary: Write minimal, evergreen code comments that explain complex logic without documenting obvious behavi...
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found repeated path traversal sequences on SKILL.md line 26 and weak cryptography indicators on lines 3 and 8. The weak cryptography findings are false positives in prose, but the parent-directory link is a real elevated risk because it instructs the agent to read outside the skill folder.

medium_findings.title: Parent Directory Reference in Skill Instructions
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 26 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/global/commenting.md. This leaves the skill directory and may cause an agent to read unreviewed parent-directory content. The context suggests shared documentation reuse, not confirmed malware or data theft.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory traversal is directly visible on line 26. Confidence is high for filesystem risk, but lower for malicious intent because the target appears to be a documentation standard.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography indicators at SKILL.md lines 3 and 8. These lines contain natural language describing code comments and coding standards. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose in the skill description and overview. There is no code path or configuration that uses a weak cryptographic algorithm.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Path Traversal Sequence
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses a ../../../ relative link in SKILL.md line 26. Parent-directory references can expand the trusted content boundary for marketplace skills.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The traversal sequence is directly present in the file. The risk is contextual because the link points to documentation rather than executable code.

content.value_statement: Teams often add comments that repeat code, age quickly, or hide unclear design. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to write concise comments that explain durable intent.
actual_capabilities: Guides comment decisions for PHP, TypeScript, JavaScript, and general code files.
Promotes comments that explain non-obvious business logic and design intent.
Supports PHPDoc, JSDoc, and TSDoc guidance for functions, methods, and data structures.
Encourages refactoring names and structure before adding explanatory comments.
Helps remove stale comments about temporary fixes or recent changes.
limitations: Does not generate project-specific documentation rules beyond the referenced guidance.
Does not verify whether referenced external standards files exist.
Does not replace human review for complex domain or compliance comments.
May need repository context to judge whether a comment is truly necessary.

use_cases.title: Review Comments Before Merge
use_cases.description: Check whether new comments explain intent instead of repeating obvious code behavior.

use_cases.title: Document Complex Logic
use_cases.description: Add concise comments around algorithms, business rules, and unusual tradeoffs.

use_cases.title: Clean Stale Documentation
use_cases.description: Find comments that describe past changes, temporary fixes, or outdated implementation details.

prompt_templates.title: Check Comment Need
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this function and tell me where comments are useful. Prefer clearer names or structure when a comment would only repeat the code.

prompt_templates.title: Add Durable Comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Add minimal comments to this code. Explain only non-obvious business rules, algorithm choices, and long-term design constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Doc Blocks
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these doc blocks for accuracy and value. Keep useful type or shape details, and remove wording that duplicates the signature.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Comment-Heavy Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this comment-heavy module toward self-documenting code. Rename symbols, simplify structure, and keep only comments that explain durable intent.

output_examples.input: A function contains several comments that describe each assignment and branch.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend removing comments that restate the code and keeping one comment for the non-obvious business rule.

output_examples.input: A PHP method accepts a nested array with a complex shape.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest a focused PHPDoc block that documents the array shape and avoids repeating the method name.

output_examples.input: A module contains comments about a temporary workaround from a past release.
output_examples.output: The skill would flag the comments as stale unless the workaround is still active and important for future maintainers.

best_practices: Use comments to explain why code exists, not what each line does.
Refactor confusing names or structure before adding explanatory comments.
Keep doc blocks accurate, concise, and focused on durable contracts.
anti_patterns: Do not add comments that narrate obvious assignments, loops, or conditionals.
Do not keep comments that describe recent changes after the change is complete.
Do not use comments to compensate for unclear names that can be improved.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants write and review minimal code comments that explain durable intent.

faq.question: Which languages does it cover?
faq.answer: It explicitly mentions PHP, TypeScript, and JavaScript, but its comment guidance can apply to other code files.

faq.question: Does it create full project documentation?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on inline comments and doc blocks inside code.

faq.question: Can it remove existing comments?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide removal of stale, redundant, or temporary comments.

faq.question: Does it support PHPDoc and JSDoc?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for PHPDoc, JSDoc, and TSDoc comments.

faq.question: What security concern was found?
faq.answer: The skill references a parent-directory file path, so publishers should verify that external content before distribution.
